title: Microsoft Q2 FY2026 Earnings: Revenue $69.6 Billion
content: REDMOND, Wash., January 29, 2026 — Microsoft Corporation today announced the following results for the quarter ended December 31, 2025. Revenue was $69.6 billion, increasing 12% year-over-year. Operating income was $31.7 billion, increasing 17%. Net income was $24.1 billion, increasing 10%. Diluted earnings per share was $3.23, increasing 10%. Satya Nadella, chairman and CEO of Microsoft, said: 'Our AI transformation is fundamentally changing how work gets done. Azure AI services grew 33% and we now have 85 million Copilot monthly active users.' The Intelligent Cloud segment contributed $25.5 billion in revenue, led by Azure. GitHub Copilot reached 1.8 million paid enterprise seats, up from 1.3 million a year ago. Microsoft 365 Commercial products and cloud services revenue grew 15%, driven by Copilot adoption and ARPU expansion. Teams has 320 million monthly active users. On January 15, 2026, Microsoft's board declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.83 per share, marking the 17th consecutive year of dividend increases. Remaining performance obligation (RPO) stands at $298 billion, up 21% year-over-year. The company returned $9.0 billion to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends in the quarter. Full-year FY2025 revenue was $245.1 billion; the company guided Q3 FY2026 revenue of $68.7–69.7 billion. Microsoft employs approximately 228,000 people globally. The company is headquartered in Redmond, Washington, and listed on the NASDAQ under the ticker MSFT.
